schema_note: v2 adds optional 'years' on stock_parts rows (omit/null = all listed years) and optional 'year_notes' on bikes. Cross-brand shared Bosch parts keep identical catalog_name strings so they merge; year scoping is carried by the per-bike 'years' field rather than baking bike-specific years into shared names. CRITICAL fitment rule verified this pass: Bosch Smart System (2022+) batteries and displays do NOT interchange with the older BDU2xx/BDU3xx/BDU4xx-era parts; they are separate catalog rows.

bikes.motor: Bosch Active Line 40Nm mid-drive (2019-2023, Gen 1-2); Bosch Active Line Plus 50Nm Smart System (2024+, Gen 3)
bikes.battery: Bosch PowerPack 400 external (2019-2023); Bosch Compact PowerTube 400 internal (2024+)
bikes.year_notes: Three generations verified. Gen 1 (MY2019-2021) and Gen 2 (MY2022-2023) share the same e-system: Bosch Active Line 40Nm, external PowerPack 400, Purion display, 2A compact charger (EBR 2020 review spec sheet; 99spokes 2023 Verve+ 2 Gen 2 still lists Bontrager H5 700x45c and the same system). Gen 3 (MY2024+) moved to the Bosch Smart System: Active Line Plus 50Nm, internal 400Wh Compact PowerTube with optional 250Wh PowerMore extender, Purion 200 display, Shimano CUES (source: trekbikes.com/us/en_US/verve-plus-generations/). v1 years '2019-2023' corrected to 2019-2025. v1 charger claim (4A standard) corrected to 2A compact per EBR spec sheet. v1 tire claim (E6 Hard-Case Lite) not supported for Gen 1-2; both EBR 2020 and 99spokes 2023 Gen 2 list Bontrager H5 700x45c.

stock_parts.comp_note: Gen 1-2 only; verified on EBR 2020 review ('Bosch Purion, Fixed, Backlit LCD') and 99spokes 2023 Gen 2. New classic Purion listings $60-135 on eBay; could not confirm 3+ used solds, estimated low at $60. Pre-Smart-System part; does NOT fit Gen 3 (2024+).

upgrade.name: Bosch Kiox BUI330 display
upgrade.note: Color screen and ride data the Purion never had. Fits the pre-smart BDU2xx/BDU3xx systems on 2019-2023 bikes only.

stock_parts.comp_note: Gen 3 (Smart System) only, per Trek's Verve+ generations page. New Purion 200 selling at $171 with 29 sold on eBay (new units, not used takeoffs), so used takeoff estimated at $120. Smart System part; does not fit 2019-2023 bikes.

upgrade.name: Bosch Kiox 300
upgrade.note: Verified $124-172 street. Smart System color display; drop-in for Purion 200 bikes.

stock_parts.comp_note: CORRECTION from v1 (which claimed the 4A standard): EBR 2020 spec sheet lists a '1.3 lb 2 Amp Compact Charger'. Gen 3 also ships a 2A compact (Smart System era). 2A compacts trade below the 4A standard's $50-135 sold band; estimated $40.

upgrade.name: Bosch standard 4A charger
upgrade.note: Halves charge time on the 400Wh pack. Confirm charger-generation match for 2024+ Smart System bikes before selling.

stock_parts.comp_note: Gen 1-2 external pack, verified on EBR 2020 ('Bosch PowerPack 400, Samsung 18650 cells, 396Wh'). New retails $675-799; used listings exist but no confirmed solds, estimated low at $250. Lithium pack, ground shipping only. Pre-smart part: does NOT fit 2024+ Gen 3 frames.

upgrade.name: Bosch PowerPack 500
upgrade.note: Same mount on 2019-2023 bikes, 25 percent more range. The standard fix for range complaints.

stock_parts.catalog_name: Bosch Compact PowerTube 400 Smart System battery (takeoff)
stock_parts.comp_note: Gen 3 internal 400Wh pack per Trek's generations page ('internal 400Wh battery and optional 250Wh PowerMore Range Extender'). Smart System part, not interchangeable with PowerPack-era packs. No used solds found; estimated in line with PowerPack 400. Lithium, ground shipping only. Range upgrades on this bike go through the Bosch PowerMore 250 extender, not a bigger main pack.
Verified on EBR 2020 spec sheet. No evidence of a saddle change across generations, so not split. Stock foam saddles trade cheap and slow; floor pricing.

upgrade.name: Brooks B17
upgrade.note: The classic commuter saddle. Breaks in to your backside and outlasts the bike.

stock_parts.comp_note: CORRECTION from v1 (which listed E6 Hard-Case Lite): EBR 2020 and 99spokes 2023 Gen 2 both list Bontrager H5 700x45c with Hard-Case Ultimate protection. Gen 3 (2024+) tire spec not verified; no row created without evidence. Used OEM tires move slow and cheap; floor per tire.

upgrade.name: Schwalbe Marathon E-Plus 27.5x2.0
upgrade.note: Verified $63-74 street. The flat-proof commuter default; buy once, forget tires exist.

stock_parts.comp_note: CORRECTION from v1 (which listed Wellgo): EBR 2020 spec sheet lists 'Bontrager Satellite City, Molded Plastic with Reflectors'. No real resale market for stock plastic platforms; bundle filler.

upgrade.name: Shimano PD-EH500
upgrade.note: Flat on one side, SPD on the other. The commuter pedal that ends the pedal debate.

stock_parts.comp_note: EBR 2020 spec: 'Verve+ Comfort Suspension, 30mm travel, alloy w/ steel core, preload adjust, 31.6mm'. No evidence of change across years. Basic spring posts trade $15-25 used; estimated low.

upgrade.name: Cane Creek Thudbuster ST G4
upgrade.note: Parallel linkage instead of a pogo spring. Night and day on chewed-up pavement.

bikes.motor: Bosch Performance Line CX 75Nm (MY2020, re-rated 85Nm by firmware for 2021-2022); Bosch Smart System Performance Line CX 85Nm (2023+, Gen 2)
bikes.battery: Bosch PowerTube 500 (2020-2022); Bosch Smart System PowerTube 500 or 625 depending on config (2023+)
bikes.year_notes: Two generations. Gen 1 (MY2020-2022): Bosch Performance Line CX (Gen 4) launched at 75Nm for MY20 and was firmware-bumped to 85Nm from MY21 - same physical BDU450 unit; Purion display; PowerTube 500 (EBR 2020 spec sheet: 'Bosch Performance Line CX (MY20), 75Nm; PowerTube 500; Purion; 4A charger; Suntour Mobie25; Schwalbe G-One 27.5x2.25; Racktime rack'). CORRECTION: v1 called the Gen 1 motor 'Performance Line 65Nm' - it was actually the CX. Gen 2 (MY2023+): Bosch Smart System, CX 85Nm, Purion 200 display + LED remote, Shimano CUES drivetrain, MIK rack (electricbikereport Gen 2 launch article; 99spokes 2024; UK retail lists 625Wh configs while US launch article says 500Wh - both exist, check the frame sticker).

stock_parts.comp_note: Gen 1 only, verified on EBR 2020 spec sheet. Shared Bosch part with other pre-smart bikes; new units $60-135, no 3+ confirmed used solds, estimated low at $60.

upgrade.name: Bosch Kiox BUI330 display
upgrade.note: The common Purion swap on Gen-1 (pre-smart) Allants. Color screen plus real ride metrics.

stock_parts.stock_part: Bosch Purion 200 display with LED remote
stock_parts.comp_note: Gen 2 Smart System only (electricbikereport Gen 2 launch; retailer specs). New Purion 200 $171 with 29 sold on eBay (new-unit sales); used takeoff estimated $120.

upgrade.name: Bosch Kiox 300
upgrade.note: Verified $124-172 street. Smart System drop-in upgrade for Purion 200 bikes.

stock_parts.comp_note: Gen 1 pack, verified on EBR 2020 (482.4Wh measured). Forum asks ~300 GBP, eBay listings $350-500, no confirmed solds - floor estimate. Pre-smart PowerTube: does NOT fit 2023+ Smart System frames. Lithium, ground shipping only.

upgrade.name: Bosch PowerTube 625
upgrade.note: Pre-smart 625 drops into most Gen-1 PowerTube 500 frames for a 25 percent range bump. Confirm frame fitment first.

stock_parts.stock_part: Bosch Smart System PowerTube battery (500 or 625Wh by config)
stock_parts.comp_note: Gen 2 ships Smart System PowerTube - US launch coverage says 500Wh, UK/EU retail lists 625Wh configs; verify the Wh sticker on intake and relabel row if it's the 500. New smart 625 runs ~$975; no used solds confirmed, estimated $400 floor. Smart System pack: NOT interchangeable with 2020-2022 PowerTubes. Lithium, ground shipping only.
Gen 1, verified on EBR 2020 spec sheet (4 Amp). eBay 4A listings with 156 and 17 units sold, $50-135 range across variants. Steady replacement demand.

Gen 2 ships the Smart System-era 4A charger. Priced in line with the pre-smart 4A sold band but no smart-specific solds isolated; estimated. Verify plug/generation compatibility before cross-selling to pre-2023 owners.

Verified on EBR 2020 ('Mobie25 coil, 50mm travel'). Gen 2 also runs a lockout suspension fork but the exact model was not verified - no split row without evidence. Thin used market, no solds found, estimated $60.

CORRECTION from v1 (which listed Super Moto-X): EBR 2020 spec sheet lists 'Schwalbe G-One, 27.5x2.25'. Gen 2 tire spec not verified. Name-brand tire so takeoffs sell, but no confirmed solds; estimated low per tire with good tread.

upgrade.name: Schwalbe Marathon E-Plus 27.5x2.0
upgrade.note: Trades the G-One's speed for near total flat protection. Commuters make this swap at first flat.

stock_parts.comp_note: Verified on EBR 2020 spec sheet; no evidence of change on Gen 2, so not split. Cheap and slow on the used market; floor pricing.

upgrade.name: Brooks B17
upgrade.note: Leather that molds to you over 500 miles. The upgrade every commuter forum recommends.

stock_parts.comp_note: Gen 1 rack verified as Racktime-made alloy, MIK-compatible, 25kg rating (EBR 2020); Gen 2 retains an MIK-compatible rack per retailer specs. Real crash-replacement demand but no confirmed solds; estimated low.
Pedal make not confirmed in dated spec sheets; carried from v1. No real resale market for stock platforms either way.

upgrade.name: Shimano PD-EH500
upgrade.note: Dual-sided commuter pedal. Grippy flat for errands, SPD for the long haul.

bikes.year_notes: The 2022 full redesign defines this listing: Specialized 2.0 motor (250W, 70Nm), U2-710 710Wh pack, MasterMind TCD display - stable across 2022-2026 model years (Specialized product pages; 99spokes 2025/2026 entries; electricbikereport 2024 review). No component split found within these years. IMPORTANT: a pre-2022 'Turbo Vado 4.0' (2019-2021) also exists but is a different platform - Specialized 1.2 motor, 604Wh battery, older TCD/Blokset electronics - and NONE of its e-parts interchange with this generation. If a pre-2022 Vado arrives, grade it as a separate bike; do not attach these rows.

stock_parts.comp_note: Specialized labels this family MasterMind TCD / TCD-w / TCD2 across years - same 2022+ MasterMind platform part. New TCD-w 2 retails ~$260; no confirmed used solds, estimated low at $120. Strong replacement demand since nothing else fits.

710Wh across all 2022+ model years (Specialized pages, electricbikereport 2024). New sells $783 at LordGun. No used solds found; estimated low at $400 for a healthy pack. Lithium, ground shipping only, proprietary so demand is captive. Does NOT fit 2019-2021 Vados (604Wh platform).
Proprietary Rosenberger-plug charger, unchanged across 2022+ years. Real lost-charger replacement demand. No confirmed solds found, estimated low at $60.
No evidence of a saddle change across 2022-2026. Specialized saddles carry brand value used but no solds confirmed. Estimated low.

upgrade.name: Brooks C17 Cambium
upgrade.note: Rubber Brooks, zero break-in, shrugs off rain. Right saddle for an all-weather Vado.

stock_parts.comp_note: Reflective-wall 650b OEM tire, consistent across the 2022+ generation. Sells but slowly; no solds found, low estimate per tire.

upgrade.name: Schwalbe Marathon E-Plus 27.5x2.0
upgrade.note: Verified $63-74 street. Heavier, slower, and you will never fix a flat in the rain again.

stock_parts.comp_note: Model-specific rack with replacement demand from crash repairs. No solds found, estimated low.
Used grips are near worthless unless mint. Floor pricing.

upgrade.name: Ergon GP1 Evo grips
upgrade.note: Wing-shaped support that kills hand numbness on long commutes. Cheapest comfort upgrade there is.

stock_parts.comp_note: No meaningful resale market for stock platforms.

upgrade.name: Shimano PD-EH500
upgrade.note: Flat plus SPD in one pedal. Standard first upgrade on a 28 mph commuter.

bikes.year_notes: The 2022 redesign put the Como on the same MasterMind platform as the Vado: 2.0E motor (250W, 50Nm), U2-530 530Wh, MasterMind TCD2 stem-mounted 2-inch color display - verified identical on Specialized's 2022 and 2023 product pages. No spec change found across 2022-2025; no split. Display naming confirmed: it is the TCD2 MasterMind (stem-mounted), the same TCD family as the Vado, so the shared display row stands. Pre-2022 Como (1.2E motor, 604Wh) is a different platform with non-interchangeable parts - grade separately if received.

stock_parts.comp_note: Specialized 2022/2023 spec: 'Turbo Connect Display 2 (TCD2) MasterMind, stem-mounted, 2 inch color'. Same MasterMind TCD family as the Vado 4.0 entry - shared row. New ~$260, no confirmed used solds, estimated low at $120.
530Wh confirmed on Specialized 2022/2023 pages. No direct used solds found; scaled down from the U2-710 estimate ($400) by capacity. Lithium, ground shipping only.

upgrade.name: Specialized U2-710 battery
upgrade.note: Same mount on this generation's frames, 34 percent more range. The takeoff 530 funds part of it.

stock_parts.comp_note: Shared proprietary charger with the Vado. No confirmed solds, estimated low at $60.
Wide comfort saddle, sells used but slow. No solds confirmed, low estimate. No evidence of change across years.

upgrade.name: Brooks B67
upgrade.note: Sprung leather made for upright bikes. Exactly what a Como rider actually wants.

stock_parts.comp_note: OEM 650b reflective tire, consistent across the generation. No solds found, estimated low per tire.

upgrade.name: Schwalbe Marathon E-Plus 27.5x2.0
upgrade.note: Flat protection worth every gram on a bike this heavy.

stock_parts.comp_note: Integrated rack with crash-replacement demand. No solds found, estimated low.
Same shared part as the Vado entry. Floor pricing.

upgrade.name: Ergon GP1 Evo grips
upgrade.note: Palm support for upright riding position. Cheap fix for numb hands.

bikes.motor: Bosch Performance Line 65Nm (2020-2022; C380+ trim ran Performance Line Speed 85Nm); Bosch Smart System Performance Line 75Nm (2023+)
bikes.battery: Bosch PowerTube 500 (2020-2022); Bosch Smart System PowerTube 625 (2023+)
bikes.year_notes: Clear 2023 generation break. Pre-2023 (EBR 2021 C380 review + EBR 2022 C380+ review): C380 = Performance Line 65Nm, PowerTube 500 (482Wh measured), Bosch PURION display, 4A standard charger, Schwalbe Energizer Plus 28x1.75; the C380+ trim upgraded to Performance Line Speed 85Nm (Class 3), Suntour Mobie45 80mm fork, Ergon GP1 grips, Selle Royal Essenza+ - but kept the Purion and PowerTube 500. CORRECTIONS to v1: display was Purion not Intuvia, motor was 65Nm (or 85Nm Speed on the plus) not 75Nm, for these years. 2023+ (Gazelle/Bosch pages, J.C. Lind 2023 listing): Bosch Smart System - next-gen Performance Line 75Nm, Kiox 300 display, PowerTube 625, tires widened to 2.0 inch. Enviolo C380 stepless hub + Gates belt unchanged throughout (near zero drivetrain takeoffs).

stock_parts.comp_note: CORRECTION from v1 (which listed Intuvia): both EBR reviews (2021 C380, 2022 C380+) list Bosch Purion. Shared pre-smart Bosch part; new $60-135, no 3+ used solds, estimated low at $60.

upgrade.name: Bosch Kiox BUI330 display
upgrade.note: Color screen on the same pre-smart BDU3xx/BDU4xx plug. Common swap for riders who want real data.

stock_parts.comp_note: Smart System years only (Gazelle US page, J.C. Lind 2023 listing: Kiox 300, eBike Flow app). New street $124-172; no confirmed used solds, estimated $100.
Verified on both EBR reviews (482.4Wh measured). Forum asks ~300 GBP, eBay listings $350-500, no confirmed solds - floor estimate. Pre-smart pack; does NOT fit 2023+ frames. Ground shipping only.

upgrade.name: Bosch PowerTube 625
upgrade.note: Pre-smart 625 fits most 2020-2022 frames for a range bump. Verify fitment by frame year.

stock_parts.comp_note: 2023+ smart-system pack (Gazelle/Bosch pages; J.C. Lind 2023: 'battery increased to 625Wh from 500Wh'). New ~$975; no used solds confirmed, estimated $400 floor. NOT interchangeable with pre-2023 PowerTubes. Lithium, ground shipping only.
Verified on both EBR reviews (4A, 1.6 lb). eBay listings with 156 and 17 units sold, $50-135 across variants.
Smart System-era 4A charger on 2023+ bikes. Priced in line with the pre-smart 4A sold band; no smart-specific solds isolated. Verify generation compatibility before cross-selling.

stock_parts.stock_part: Selle Royal saddle (Loire Gel on C380, Essenza+ on C380+)
stock_parts.comp_note: Trim variance, not year variance: EBR 2021 base C380 = Selle Royal Loire Gel; EBR 2022 C380+ = Selle Royal Essenza+. Both decent OEM Selle Royals with similar resale; one row kept. No solds confirmed, estimated low.

upgrade.name: Brooks B17
upgrade.note: The Dutch bike deserves the English saddle. Lasts decades.

stock_parts.comp_note: Verified 28x1.75 on both EBR reviews (2020-2022); 2023+ bikes run the 2.0-inch width (J.C. Lind 2023). Same tire family, width differs by year - note width when listing. E-rated Schwalbe so takeoffs sell, but no confirmed solds; low estimate per tire.

upgrade.name: Schwalbe Marathon E-Plus 27.5x2.0
upgrade.note: Step up from Energizer to the flat-proof Marathon casing. Verified $63-74 street.

stock_parts.comp_note: Trim variance: EBR 2022 C380+ = Ergon GP1 with lock ring; EBR 2021 base C380 = generic padded faux-leather locking grips (near-zero value). Price this row only when the takeoff is genuinely Ergon. No solds confirmed.
Crash-replacement demand from Gazelle owners, thin supply in the US. No solds found, estimated low. No evidence of year change.

stock_parts.stock_part: SR Suntour Mobie45 fork 80mm (C380+ / later years); base 2021 C380 ran a rigid alloy fork with 40mm monoshock headset suspension
stock_parts.comp_note: Trim variance verified: EBR 2022 C380+ = Suntour Mobie45 80mm with lockout; EBR 2021 base C380 = alloy fork with internal 40mm monoshock (no Suntour takeoff on those). Only price this row when a Suntour fork actually comes off. Thin used market, no solds found, estimated $60.

bikes.motor: Bosch Active Line Plus 50Nm (2019-2023); Bosch Smart System Active Line Plus 50Nm (2024+)
bikes.battery: Bosch PowerPack 400 rack-mounted (2019-2020); Bosch PowerTube 500 in-frame (approx 2021-2023); Bosch PowerPack 545 on-tube Smart System (2024+)
bikes.year_notes: Three battery/display phases verified. 2019 (EBR review spec sheet): Active Line Plus 50Nm, RACK-MOUNTED PowerPack 400 (396Wh), Purion, 2A compact charger, Selle Royal Herz, Schwalbe Energizer Plus 28x1.75, Gazelle rack, Suntour CR7V fork. Mid-generation the battery moved into the downtube as a PowerTube 500 (dealer listings; exact switch year not pinned - verify battery location on intake photos, approx 2021). Current bike (Gazelle US site, 2024+): Bosch SMART SYSTEM Active Line Plus 50Nm, on-tube PowerPack 545Wh, Purion 200 display, 2A compact charger, Continental Contact Plus tires, DDK saddle, MIK aluminum carrier. Do not conflate with the Medeo T9 CITY trim - that is a different bike (Active Line 40Nm, rack PowerPack 400, Big Apple tires; EBR 2022 City HMB review + electricbikereport 2024 City review).

stock_parts.comp_note: Verified on EBR 2019 spec sheet; pre-smart part. New $60-135 on eBay, no 3+ confirmed used solds, estimated low at $60.

upgrade.name: Bosch Kiox BUI330 display
upgrade.note: Fits the pre-smart BDU3xx system on 2019-2023 bikes. Color screen, real data.

stock_parts.comp_note: Current Gazelle US spec: Purion 200 (non-removable, Smart System). New $171 with 29 sold on eBay (new units); used takeoff estimated $120.

upgrade.name: Bosch Kiox 300
upgrade.note: Verified $124-172 street. Smart System drop-in for Purion 200 Medeos; older BDU3xx bikes take the Kiox BUI330 instead.

stock_parts.comp_note: CORRECTION from v1 (which listed PowerTube 500 for all years): EBR 2019 spec sheet shows a rack-mounted PowerPack 400 (396Wh). Switch year to the in-frame PowerTube is approximate - confirm by battery location on intake. New PowerPack 400 retails $675-799; no confirmed used solds, estimated $250. Lithium, ground shipping only.

upgrade.name: Bosch PowerPack 500
upgrade.note: Same rack/frame mount family, 25 percent more range.

stock_parts.comp_note: Mid-generation in-frame pack (dealer listings; the 400Wh rack pack moved into the downtube per multiple retail descriptions - switch year approximate). Same evidence base as other PowerTube 500 rows: asks $350-500, no confirmed solds, floor estimate. Pre-smart pack. Ground shipping only.

upgrade.name: Bosch PowerTube 625
upgrade.note: Pre-smart 625 in the same slot in most frames. Check frame year fitment.

stock_parts.catalog_name: Bosch PowerPack 545 on-tube Smart System battery (takeoff)
stock_parts.comp_note: Current Gazelle US spec: Bosch 'Ontube' 545Wh, Smart System. No used market data yet (new SKU); estimated between the PowerTube 500 and 625 floors. NOT interchangeable with pre-2024 packs. Lithium, ground shipping only.

2A compact verified in BOTH eras (EBR 2019 spec sheet; current Gazelle US page 'Bosch Compact Charger 2A') - the one component that never changed. Trades below the 4A standard's $50-135 sold band; estimated $40. The 4A upsell remains the most predictable sale on this bike.

upgrade.name: Bosch standard 4A charger
upgrade.note: Halves the charge time. First thing Medeo owners buy. Match charger generation on 2024+ Smart System bikes.

stock_parts.stock_part: Selle Royal comfort saddle (Herz verified 2019; Essenza Plus reported later years)
stock_parts.comp_note: EBR 2019 spec sheet says Selle Royal Herz; v1's Essenza Plus claim for later years unverified - both are similar-value Selle Royal comfort OEM saddles, one row kept. 2024+ bikes switched to a DDK saddle (separate row). No solds confirmed, estimated low.

upgrade.name: Brooks B17
upgrade.note: Buy once, ride it for twenty years.

stock_parts.comp_note: Current Gazelle US spec lists a DDK saddle on the Smart System bike. Generic OEM comfort saddle, floor of the used market. No solds found.

upgrade.name: Brooks B17
upgrade.note: Buy once, ride it for twenty years.

stock_parts.comp_note: Verified on EBR 2019 spec sheet (28x1.75, G-Guard 5). Shared row with the Ultimate C380. Low estimate per tire.

upgrade.name: Schwalbe Marathon E-Plus 27.5x2.0
upgrade.note: Flat-proof casing, verified $63-74 street. The commuter no-brainer.

stock_parts.comp_note: Current Gazelle US spec: Continental Contact Plus front and rear on the Smart System bike. E-rated name-brand tire; no solds confirmed, low estimate per tire.

upgrade.name: Schwalbe Marathon E-Plus 27.5x2.0
upgrade.note: Flat-proof casing, verified $63-74 street.

stock_parts.stock_part: Gazelle rear rack (MIK carrier on 2024+ bikes)
stock_parts.comp_note: EBR 2019: Gazelle-branded rack with pannier hangers, 25kg. Current bikes: aluminum MIK-system carrier. Same replacement-demand story either way; one row kept, note MIK compatibility when listing 2024+ takeoffs. Estimated low.
Pedal make not confirmed in dated spec sheets; carried from v1. No real resale market for stock platforms.

upgrade.name: Shimano PD-EH500
upgrade.note: Flat one side, clipless the other. Sorted.

bikes.motor: Giant SyncDrive Sport (Yamaha PWseries ST) 70-80Nm (2020-2022); Giant SyncDrive Sport2 75Nm (2023+ redesign)
bikes.year_notes: Two platforms. Old platform (MY2020-2022): SyncDrive Sport powered by Yamaha PWseries ST (EBR 2020 spec sheet claims 80Nm; Giant later rated it 70Nm - same unit, ratings drifted), RideControl ONE LED button console (no screen), EnergyPak 400 (406.8Wh) on the 2020 E+ 4 GTS per EBR, with dealer/Giant pages listing EnergyPak Smart 500 on later units - switch year not fully pinned, verify the Wh sticker on intake. 3A charger with dongle adapter (EBR 2020). Redesigned platform (2023+, Smart Gateway 2.0 era, sold through 2026 per EU retail): SyncDrive Sport2 75Nm, RideControl Dash 2 color display + Ergo 3 remote, EnergyPak Smart 500, 4A Smart Charger. CORRECTIONS to v1: the Dash 2 display only exists on 2023+ bikes (older bikes have the screenless RideControl One); v1's single 75Nm/EnergyPak-500 spec blended the two platforms; 2020 saddle is Selle Royal Vivo and tires are Crosscut Gravel 2 (not Crosscut AT 2). US availability of the 2023+ E+ 4 trim is thin (EU-led); most US takeoffs will be 2020-2022 bikes.

stock_parts.stock_part: Giant RideControl One LED controller (no screen)
stock_parts.comp_note: CORRECTION from v1 (which listed Dash 2 for all years): EBR 2020 spec sheet shows the RideControl One fixed LED console - battery/assist dots only, no screen. Thin Giant-only replacement market, conservative $30 estimate, no solds found.

Redesigned 2023+ platform only (Smart Gateway 2.0; 1.5-inch color display + control unit, per Giant/ebike24 documentation). No sold listings found; conservative $50 estimate on replacement demand alone.

EBR 2020 E+ 4 GTS spec sheet: EnergyPak 400, 36V 11.3Ah, 406.8Wh, side-release integrated. Smaller pack than v1 claimed for this year. No used solds found; estimated below the Smart 500 row. Ground shipping only.

Giant/dealer pages list EnergyPak Smart 500 on later old-platform units and on the 2023+ redesign (ebike24 2026 listing). Exact 400-to-500 switch year not pinned - verify the Wh sticker. New replacement ~$760 per forum reports; no used solds found, estimated $300 given a smaller buyer pool than Bosch. Ground shipping only.
EBR 2020 spec sheet: 1.9 lb 3A charger with dongle adapter. Proprietary plug, captive replacement demand. No solds found, estimated $50 in line with the Bosch 4A sold band.
Giant pairs the EnergyPak Smart packs with the 4A Smart Charger (Giant marketing pages). Proprietary plug, captive demand. No solds found, estimated $50.
CORRECTION from v1 (which listed a generic 'Giant Comfort saddle'): EBR 2020 spec sheet lists Selle Royal Vivo. Later years unverified. Floor of the used market, no solds found.

upgrade.name: Brooks B17
upgrade.note: The saddle that turns a trekking bike into a touring bike.

stock_parts.comp_note: CORRECTION from v1 (which listed Crosscut AT 2): EBR 2020 GTS spec sheet lists Crosscut Gravel 2 700x45c tubeless-ready. The AT 2 appears on other Explore trims/years but was not confirmed on the E+ 4. House-brand tire, weak used demand; floor estimate per tire.

upgrade.name: Schwalbe Marathon E-Plus 700c
upgrade.note: Same flat-proof casing in 700c fitment. Check clearance against the stock 45mm width.

stock_parts.comp_note: HONESTY FLAG: the 2020 E+ 4 GTS shipped with rack BOSSES only, no rack (EBR 2020) - do not expect a rack takeoff from every unit. Some STA/later builds include one. When present, crash-replacement demand exists. No solds found, estimated low.
Verified Wellgo K79 alloy cage on EBR 2020 spec sheet. Still no meaningful resale market for stock platforms.

upgrade.name: Shimano PD-EH500
upgrade.note: The commuter pedal everyone lands on eventually.

bikes.year_notes: NO e-system year splits - verified consistent across 2021, 2022, 2023, and 2024 listings (Bosch ePowered page, Cannondale page, Mack Cycle 2021 listing, opticycles 2023 and 2024 reviews): Bosch Active Line 40Nm, PowerTube 400, Purion display, 2A compact charger every year. The only year-to-year variance found is the mechanical drivetrain, where supply chains forced swaps between Shimano Altus and microSHIFT Marvo 9-speed parts on different production runs - note the actual derailleur on intake, but it does not affect the takeoff rows below. Pre-smart Bosch platform throughout its run.

stock_parts.comp_note: Purion verified on all model years (Bosch/Cannondale/opticycles). Shared pre-smart Bosch part; new $60-135, no 3+ used solds, estimated low at $60.

upgrade.name: Bosch Kiox BUI330 display
upgrade.note: Straight swap on the pre-smart BDU2xx system. Color screen, real data.

stock_parts.comp_note: PowerTube 400 verified on all model years. No direct used solds found; scaled below the PowerTube 500 floor estimate by capacity. Pre-smart pack. Lithium, ground shipping only.
2A charger verified on all model years (Bosch ePowered spec). Estimated $40 off the 4A's $50-135 sold band. The 4A upsell story holds every year of this bike.

upgrade.name: Bosch standard 4A charger
upgrade.note: Cuts charge time in half. Every Neo owner wants one by month two.

stock_parts.comp_note: Stock foam saddle, floor of the used market. No solds found. No evidence of year change.

upgrade.name: Brooks B17
upgrade.note: The upgrade the whole forum tells them to buy.

stock_parts.comp_note: Budget OEM tire with the weakest resale on this list. Floor estimate per tire. No evidence of year change.

upgrade.name: Schwalbe Marathon E-Plus 27.5x2.0
upgrade.note: Verified $63-74 street. Biggest single reliability upgrade on this bike.

stock_parts.comp_note: Used grips are near worthless. Floor pricing.

upgrade.name: Ergon GP1 Evo grips
upgrade.note: Forty dollars to fix numb hands. Easy yes.

stock_parts.comp_note: No real resale market for stock platforms.

upgrade.name: Shimano PD-EH500
upgrade.note: Dual-sided and durable. Done.
